questions.question: A non-uniform beam is $2.0\text{ m}$ long. It balances at its midpoint when a $60\text{ N}$ weight is $0.2\text{ m}$ from the pivot on one side and a $40\text{ N}$ weight is $0.6\text{ m}$ from the pivot on the other side. What is the moment produced by the beam's weight about the pivot?
options: $12\text{ N m}$
$0\text{ N m}$
$24\text{ N m}$
$36\text{ N m}$
questions.explanation:
ACW moment from weight 1 $= 60 \times 0.2 = 12\text{ N m}$. CW moment from weight 2 $= 40 \times 0.6 = 24\text{ N m}$. For equilibrium, the beam's own weight must provide an ACW moment of $24 - 12 = 12\text{ N m}$. 

**Common mistake:** Assuming weight always acts through the pivot in every balance problem.

questions.question: In the experiment to locate the centre of gravity of a lamina, why is it important to make the holes near the edge and as far apart as possible?
options: To increase the accuracy of the intersection point
To make the lamina lighter and easier to hang
To move the centre of gravity closer to the edge
To ensure the plumb line does not touch the retort stand
questions.explanation:
Having holes far apart creates lines that intersect at larger angles, making the intersection point (the COG) easier to identify accurately. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking the position of holes actually changes where the COG is located.

questions.question: A uniform metre rule is pivoted at the $70\text{ cm}$ mark. It balances horizontally when a $2.5\text{ N}$ weight is hung at the $80\text{ cm}$ mark. Calculate the weight of the ruler.
questions.explanation:
The rule's weight $W$ acts at the $50\text{ cm}$ mark ($20\text{ cm}$ from pivot). The $2.5\text{ N}$ load is $10\text{ cm} from the pivot$. $2W \times 20 = 2.5 \times 10 Rightarrow 20W = 25 Rightarrow W = 1.25\text{ N}$. 

**Common mistake:** Using the full $50\text{ cm}$ or $70\text{ cm}$ length as the distance instead of the distance from the pivot.

questions.question: A tall cylinder is half-filled with water and placed on a plank. As the plank is tilted, at what point will the cylinder tip over?
options: When the vertical line from its centre of gravity falls outside the edge of its base
As soon as the plank reaches an angle of $45^{circ}$
When the water surface is no longer parallel to the base
When the centre of gravity reaches the highest possible point
questions.explanation:
Regardless of the contents, an object topples the moment its centre of gravity's line of action passes the edge of the base. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking the liquid's movement prevents toppling; it actually shifts the COG and usually makes it topple sooner.

questions.question: Which change would most effectively increase the stability of a racing car?
options: Increasing the track width (distance between wheels) and lowering the engine position
Using a lighter material for the chassis and a heavier spoiler on top
Increasing the wheel diameter and reducing the fuel tank size
Moving the driver's seat further back and higher up
questions.explanation:
Lowering the engine lowers the COG, and increasing the track width increases the base area, both maximizing stability. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking speed-related parts like spoilers always improve balance/stability in a stationary sense.
